metric: cortex_on_exact (mean +/- 95% CI over seeds); OFF = cortex-off text-only control
honest_reading: Cortex ON >> text-only OFF for both codes (bearing 71-81% vs 11%; distance ~46-58% vs 14-17%): the LLM reads spatial answers out of the cortex channel, not the text. This is the robust, primary result.

grid vs place is NOT statistically separable at n=3 -- CIs are enormous (distance grid +/-38-42%). The single-seed 'grid 95/88/85 vs place 62/46/40' on distance did NOT replicate; multi-seed it is ~50% with +/-40% bars (a lucky seed).
Bearing is the one suggestive signal: grid is tighter/higher/flat to 3x (80/81/71) vs place noisy/lower (53/43/47), but CIs still overlap at n=3.

Distance fine-tuning is high-variance at n=3 (6-class, 1 epoch, early-stopping); resolving grid vs place needs n>=8, and -- consistent with the CPU characterization -- may well remain a tie or a modest bearing-only advantage.
